patch.value: GH-529: init --agent-guidance emits run-tests.mjs linked-test example that may not exist in the target repo

pm init --agent-guidance add writes an auto-managed AGENTS.md quickstart block whose linked-test example is 'pm test <id> --add command="node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- ..."'. That script is pm-cli-repo-specific; in arbitrary host repos the trusted onboarding guidance points at a non-existent command, causing confusion and linked-test-evidence drift. Fix direction: render a repo-agnostic placeholder template ('<your project test command>') or detect an existing test entry point (package.json scripts.test) before rendering a concrete command. Class: environment/capability-aware hint rendering — same family as pm-qljv (package-only reindex hint) and the pm-mpbb 'derive hints from actual capability' ledger; audit other init-guidance hints for repo-specific assumptions at fix time.
Generated guidance never names a command absent from the target repo (placeholder template or detected test entry); init-guidance hints audited for repo-specific assumptions; GH-529 closed
